page: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a bare metal server configured with x86_64 architecture. It features two hundred and fifty-six dedicated physical AMD EPYC 9575F CPU cores operating at a base frequency of 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with three thousand and seventy-two gigabytes of system memory, providing twelve gigabytes of RAM per physical core.

The server integrates eight NVIDIA B200 GPUs from the Blackwell family, delivering a combined total of one thousand five hundred and thirty-six gigabytes of high-bandwidth video memory. Local storage is provided by twenty-eight thousand six hundred and eight gigabytes of NVMe SSD capacity, supporting high-throughput data access. The configuration also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high density of Blackwell GPUs, massive system memory, and extensive NVMe storage, this bare metal instance represents a high-tier hardware class optimized for resource-intensive workloads. It is designed for large-scale artificial intelligence model training, complex deep learning inference, massive data science pipelines, and high-performance computing tasks that require direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
description: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for intensive computational workloads. The system features 256 dedicated AMD EPYC 9575F physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 3072 GB of system memory. For acceleration, the server incorporates eight NVIDIA B200 Blackwell GPUs, providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This bare metal configuration provides direct hardware access, eliminating virtualization overhead. It is engineered for large-scale artificial intelligence model training, deep learning inference, and complex data science workloads requiring massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage.

Eight NVIDIA B200 Blackwell GPUs with 1536 GB total VRAM
